summary: One-line tooling change in tools/project.py: ProjectConfig.use_wibo now returns true only on Linux, removing Darwin/macOS from automatic wibo use. With compiler_wrapper falling back to Path("wine") when no wrapper is set on non-Windows systems, the change effectively moves macOS builds away from auto-downloaded wibo and toward wine. The PR had no C/ASM changes; decomp.dev reported GALE01 "No changes" and two reviewers approved without written feedback.

key_files.role: Changed ProjectConfig.use_wibo platform predicate from Linux-or-Darwin to Linux-only.

what_changed: Removed sys.platform == "darwin" from the use_wibo condition.
Kept the existing requirements that wibo_tag is set, the machine is one of i386/x86_64/aarch64/arm64, and no explicit wrapper is configured.
Because compiler_wrapper only selects build/tools/wibo when use_wibo() is true and otherwise falls back to Path("wine") on non-Windows systems, macOS no longer auto-selects wibo by default.
No game source, headers, assembly, or matching logic changed.
smart_moves: The platform behavior was changed at the central use_wibo gate instead of scattering macOS special cases elsewhere in build generation.
The explicit wrapper override path was preserved: if ProjectConfig.wrapper is set, use_wibo remains false and the configured wrapper can still be used.
The architecture allowlist was left untouched, minimizing the risk of changing Linux behavior while fixing macOS wrapper selection.
The PR relied on decomp.dev verification; the bot reported GALE01 "No changes", appropriate for a tooling-only wrapper selection change.
decomp_lessons: Keep host-tool decisions centralized in ProjectConfig.compiler_wrapper/use_wibo so platform behavior is easy to audit.
For decompilation infrastructure changes, verify that the game binary comparison remains unchanged; here the only recorded decomp.dev report was "No changes".
When moving a host platform from one wrapper backend to another, prefer narrowing the backend predicate rather than changing downstream compiler command construction.
Preserve user escape hatches such as an explicit wrapper path when altering default host behavior.
naming_conventions: Boolean helper name use_wibo clearly expresses the decision being gated.
Configuration fields use suffixes such as _tag and _path for downloadable tool version versus explicit path selection, e.g. wibo_tag and wrapper.
Host platform checks use sys.platform string comparisons, here sys.platform == "linux".
Machine architecture checks use platform.machine() against a tuple of accepted values: i386, x86_64, aarch64, arm64.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Not applicable: this PR did not modify C source, headers, assembly, or matching tactics.
Relevant verification tactic was negative: decomp.dev's GALE01 report said "No changes", confirming the tooling tweak did not affect output.
review_feedback: No inline review comments were present in the slice.
ribbanya approved the PR on 2026-04-25 with an empty review body.
jurrejelle approved the PR on 2026-04-25 with an empty review body.
decomp-dev[bot] commented: "Report for GALE01 ... No changes".
